message.content: {"items":[{"title":"German General Social Survey – ALLBUS 2010","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.10850","justification":"ALLBUS 2010 covers social behaviour, attitudes, political behaviour, media use, and evaluations of government and economic conditions in Germany in 2010."},{"title":"German General Social Survey – ALLBUS 2012","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.11748","justification":"Nationwide survey of the German population on social structure, social behaviour, political attitudes, media, and government/economic evaluations, fielded in 2012."},{"title":"German General Social Survey – ALLBUS 2014","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12253","justification":"Provides German data on social and political attitudes, behaviour, media usage, and views on government and economic policy for 2014."},{"title":"German General Social Survey – ALLBUS 2016","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12806","justification":"Covers political behaviour and attitudes, social behaviour, media, and assessments of economic systems and policy in Germany in 2016."},{"title":"German General Social Survey – ALLBUS 2018","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13253","justification":"Survey of German residents capturing social and political attitudes, behaviour, media, and views on government and economy for 2018."},{"title":"German Longitudinal Election Study (GLES) 2009–2013: Rolling Cross-Section Campaign Panel 2013","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12188","justification":"Though focused on the 2013 federal election, this GLES component includes detailed measures of political behaviour and attitudes, party preferences, media exposure, and evaluations of government and economic policy in Germany in early‑2010s."},{"title":"German Longitudinal Election Study (GLES) 2013: Pre- and Post-Election Cross-Section","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.11975","justification":"National election study with rich data on voting behaviour, political attitudes, media use, party and government evaluations, and economic policy perceptions in Germany around the 2013 election."},{"title":"German Longitudinal Election Study (GLES) 2017: Pre- and Post-Election Cross-Section","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12916","justification":"2017 German federal election cross-sections including political behaviour and attitudes, media consumption, views on government, parties and economic policy in Germany later in the 2010s."},{"title":"Politbarometer West 2010 (Monthly Cross-Sections)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.11009","justification":"Continuous survey series on political opinions in Germany with 2010 wave data on voting intention, party preferences, attitudes to government and economic policy, and some media-related variables."},{"title":"German Internet Panel (GIP) 2012–2019","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13536","justification":"Panel survey of the German population with repeated waves over the 2010s on political attitudes and behaviour, opinions on government and economic policy, and media/information use."}]}
